varieties.name: Gladalan Brown
varieties.origin: Armadale, Western Australia, 1920s. Bred by Glad and Alan Brown at Gladalan nursery.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized brown bulbs, 5-8cm diameter. Short day variety requiring 10-12 hours daylight.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity. Bulbs ready in 15-18 weeks.
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, Diggers Club, Queensland seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Red Creole
varieties.origin: Louisiana/Southern USA, early 1900s. Classic Cajun variety.
varieties.traits: Small-medium dark red, flat-globe bulbs, 7-10cm diameter. Short day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, disease tolerant. Matures 110 days from transplant.
varieties.availability: Love of Dirt, Australian seed suppliers, imported short-day varieties.

varieties.name: Long Tropea Red
varieties.origin: Tropea, Calabria, Italy. Ancient heirloom, cultivated for centuries.
varieties.traits: Elongated torpedo-shaped red bulbs, 12-15cm long. Also called Cipolla Rossa di Tropea.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield. Early maturing variety.
varieties.availability: Love of Dirt, specialty heirloom seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Australian Brown
varieties.origin: Australia, introduced by Burpee 1897. Originally from Spanish stock.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized amber/brownish-red skin, rounded slightly flattened bulbs. Intermediate day variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Extra early, highly uniform. Matures 100 days from transplant.
varieties.availability: Seed Collection Australia, Mary's Heirloom Seeds, Eden Brothers, Thresh Seed Co.

varieties.name: Texas Early Grano 1015Y
varieties.origin: Texas A&M University, 1980s. Developed by Dr. Leonard Pike. Named for October 15 planting date.
varieties.traits: Large yellow-skinned bulbs, 15-20cm diameter, can reach 1-2 pounds. Short day but adaptable.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yields of large bulbs. Matures 175 days from seed, 100 days from transplant.

varieties.name: Hunter River White
varieties.origin: Hunter Valley, New South Wales, Australia. Traditional Australian variety.
varieties.traits: White-skinned bulbs, medium size. Early variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields. Early maturing.
varieties.availability: Sustainable Gardening Australia, Australian heritage seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Gladalan White
varieties.origin: Australia. Short day open-pollinated variety.
varieties.traits: White bulbs, medium size. Short day type requiring 10-12 hours daylight.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields. Matures November-January depending on planting.
varieties.availability: Australian commercial onion suppliers, Queensland nurseries.

varieties.name: Early Barletta
varieties.origin: Italian heritage variety.
varieties.traits: Small white bulbs, perfect for pickling. Early variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Can be lifted at 70 days for pickling or left to mature.

varieties.name: Sweet Red
varieties.origin: Mid-season variety.
varieties.traits: Red-skinned bulbs, medium size.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields, mid-season maturity.
varieties.availability: Sustainable Gardening Australia, Australian seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Brown Spanish
varieties.origin: Spanish ancestry, late variety.
varieties.traits: Brown-skinned bulbs, round shape. Intermediate day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields for late planting.
varieties.availability: Sustainable Gardening Australia, heritage seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Borettana Yellow Cipollini
varieties.origin: Boretto, Italy, cultivated since 1400s. Traditional Italian heirloom.
varieties.traits: Flat button-shaped yellow-bronze bulbs, 5-10cm diameter, 2-3cm thick. Intermediate/long day.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield. Matures 110 days. Small uniform bulbs.
varieties.availability: Renee's Garden, Dixondale Farms, Thresh Seed Co., Fedco Seeds.

varieties.name: Red Emperor
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety developed for Australian conditions.
varieties.traits: Red-skinned bulbs. Intermediate day hybrid.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields. Grown in Riverina NSW and Tasmania.
varieties.availability: Australian commercial growers, Queensland agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Redwing
varieties.origin: Intermediate day hybrid for Australian production.
varieties.traits: Red-skinned bulbs. Intermediate day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Grown in SA, Victoria, WA. Plant August-September, harvest February-March.
varieties.availability: Commercial Australian growers.

varieties.name: Walla Walla
varieties.origin: Walla Walla, Washington, USA. Brought by French settlers from Corsica in 1800s.
varieties.traits: Large yellow-brown bulbs, 13-15cm diameter. Long day variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Large bulbs, can reach 5-6 inches diameter. Matures 90 days.

varieties.name: Ailsa Craig
varieties.origin: Scotland, 1887. Developed by David Murray, gardener for Marquis of Ailsa. Cross between Danvers Yellow and Cranston's Excelsior.
varieties.traits: Very large pale yellow-straw globes, 15-20cm diameter. Exhibition variety. Long day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Enormous bulbs 1-3 pounds each. Matures 100-110 days from transplant.

varieties.name: Yellow Danvers
varieties.origin: Danvers, Massachusetts, USA. Developed from English common yellow in 18th century.
varieties.traits: Round yellow-fleshed bulbs, 7-8cm diameter. Also known as Round Yellow Danvers.
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive, uniform. Matures 105 days.

varieties.name: Southport White Globe
varieties.origin: Southport, Connecticut, USA, 1906. Also called White Rocca, Silver Ball.
varieties.traits: Perfect round white globes, 5-8cm diameter. Long day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields. Often harvested young as pearl/pickling onion. Matures 70 days.

varieties.name: Red Wethersfield
varieties.origin: Wethersfield, Connecticut, USA. First listed 1834 as Large Red. Promoted by Comstock Ferre Seed Co.
varieties.traits: Large red bulbs, rounded to flattened. Long day variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive. Good taste. Reasonable keeper.

varieties.name: Welsh Onion 'Green Stem'
varieties.origin: Siberia and Northern China. Not from Wales - 'Welsh' from German 'walsch' meaning foreign.
varieties.traits: Non-bulbing perennial bunching type. Produces multiple side shoots.
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous, clumping. Ready 11-13 weeks. Can harvest continuously as perennial.
varieties.availability: Succeed Heirlooms Australia, local Brisbane nurseries.

varieties.name: Spring Onion / Shallot Bunching
varieties.origin: Various bunching types.
varieties.traits: Quick-growing bunching onions, minimal bulbing.
varieties.yield_notes: Can be sown directly spring through autumn.
varieties.availability: Sustainable Gardening Australia, all Queensland nurseries.

varieties.name: Arka Niketan 🌱
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore, India. Recommended for Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh coasts.
varieties.traits: Medium white bulbs, thin neck. Short day type. Early maturing.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding 19.64 t/ha. Matures 95-110 days after transplant.
varieties.availability: IARI seed centers, Kerala Agricultural University, Thrissur KVK, Badikheti online.

varieties.name: Arka Kalyan 🌱
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore. Specially recommended for Kerala, TN, AP coasts.
varieties.traits: Short day variety. Suitable for tropical conditions.
varieties.yield_notes: HIGHEST yield 21.06 t/ha in kharif trials. Matures 100-110 days.

varieties.name: Arka Pitamber
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore.
varieties.traits: Yellow-skinned bulbs, medium size with thin neck.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields with thin neck ensuring better storage.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Kerala agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Arka Kirthiman
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore. Suitable for Deccan Plateau tropical climate.
varieties.traits: Red bulbs, globe shaped.
varieties.yield_notes: Suitable for hot summer conditions.

varieties.name: Arka Bindu
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore. Developed for Deccan Plateau.
varieties.traits: Tropical climate adapted variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Performs well in very hot summers.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Karnataka and Kerala agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Arka Lalima
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore.
varieties.traits: Red variety for tropical conditions.
varieties.yield_notes: Long-day type but can be planted with irrigation in drier tropical regions.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, South Indian seed suppliers.

varieties.name: Arka Suvarna
varieties.origin: IARI Bangalore. Hill variety.
varieties.traits: Day-neutral variety for elevations 1000-1500m.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields at elevation.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Kerala highland agricultural stores.

varieties.name: Bhima Super 🧬
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released for kharif season across India including Kerala.
varieties.traits: Red round bulbs tapering toward neck. 95% single-centered bulbs. TSS 10-11%.
varieties.yield_notes: Average 20-22 t/ha kharif, 40-45 t/ha late kharif. Matures 100-105 days kharif, 110-120 days late kharif.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Kerala Agricultural University, Mahaseeds, Shine Brand Seeds, major Kerala agricultural stores.

varieties.name: Bhima Shakti 🧬
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Recently notified variety.
varieties.traits: Hybrid red variety. Good uniformity.
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yields than open-pollinated varieties.

varieties.name: Bhima Kiran 🧬
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released 2010 for multiple zones including South India.
varieties.traits: Light red bulbs. Hybrid variety. Moderately intense flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Good commercial yields.

varieties.name: Bhima Red 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released for kharif, late kharif, and rabi.
varieties.traits: Red round bulbs. TSS 10-11%. Open-pollinated.
varieties.yield_notes: 19-21 t/ha kharif, 48-52 t/ha late kharif, 30-32 t/ha rabi. Matures 105-120 days depending on season.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Kerala Agricultural University, widely available Kerala agricultural stores.

varieties.name: Bhima Dark Red 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released for kharif across 11 states including Tamil Nadu.
varieties.traits: Attractive dark red flat-globe bulbs. Very appealing color.
varieties.yield_notes: 20-22 t/ha. Matures 95-100 days after transplant.

varieties.name: Bhima Shweta 🧬
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released 2011 for rabi and kharif.
varieties.traits: Attractive white round bulbs. Thin neck. TSS 11.5%. Less than 3% doubles/bolters.
varieties.yield_notes: 35.9 t/ha rabi (national average 28.2 t/ha). Matures 110-120 days.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Kerala agricultural centers, commercial suppliers.

varieties.name: Bhima Shubra 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released 2010 for kharif and late kharif.
varieties.traits: White oval-round bulbs. Thin neck. Less than 6% bolters late kharif, 3% kharif. TSS 10.4-11.7%.
varieties.yield_notes: 24.16 t/ha kharif, 38.9 t/ha late kharif. Matures 112 days kharif, 125 days late kharif.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Kerala agricultural suppliers, commercial seed companies.

varieties.name: Bhima Light Red 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Released 2016 for rabi in Karnataka and Tamil Nadu. Notified 2018.
varieties.traits: Light red globe bulbs, 60-80g. Thin neck. TSS 12.5-13.5%. Almost free from doubles/bolters.
varieties.yield_notes: 36-40 t/ha rabi. Matures 110-120 days with uniform neck fall.

varieties.name: Bhima Safed 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune (line NRCWO-3). Suitable for multiple states including Tamil Nadu.
varieties.traits: White round-oval bulbs, 70-80g. Medium maturing 110-120 days. TSS 11-12%.
varieties.yield_notes: 18.5 t/ha average in recommended zones.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Tamil Nadu and Kerala agricultural centers.

varieties.name: Bhima Raj 🌱
varieties.origin: ICAR-DOGR Pune. Suitable for kharif, late kharif, and rabi.
varieties.traits: Dark red oval bulbs with single center. Thin neck. TSS 10-11%. Absolutely no bolters.
varieties.yield_notes: 25-30 t/ha rabi (highest potential 40-45 t/ha). Matures 120-125 days. High percentage marketable bulbs.
varieties.availability: ICAR-DOGR, Kerala and Karnataka agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Pusa Red 🌱
varieties.origin: IARI Delhi. Day-neutral short day variety.
varieties.traits: Deep red bulbs. Short day type.
varieties.yield_notes: Quick maturing 100-110 days.

varieties.name: Pusa Ratnar 🌱
varieties.origin: IARI Delhi. Creation of IARI breeding program.
varieties.traits: Pinkish-red bulbs, medium size. Day-neutral short day.
varieties.yield_notes: Quick maturing 100-110 days.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Badikheti online, major seed companies.

varieties.name: Pusa Madhavi 🌱
varieties.origin: IARI Delhi. Short day variety.
varieties.traits: Medium bulbs with good storage quality.
varieties.yield_notes: 18.96 t/ha in kharif trials. Quick maturing 100-110 days.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Badikheti, Kerala agricultural stores.

varieties.name: Pusa Riddhi
varieties.origin: IARI Delhi. Very early maturing variety.
varieties.traits: Early variety for monsoon conditions.
varieties.yield_notes: Very early maturity ideal for quick turnaround.
varieties.availability: IARI centers, Kerala agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Agrifound Dark Red 🌱
varieties.origin: NHRDF (National Horticultural Research and Development Foundation).
varieties.traits: Dark red flat-globe bulbs. Very attractive color.
varieties.yield_notes: 18.06 t/ha in kharif. Moderately high yield. Suitable for rainy season.

varieties.name: Agrifound Light Red 🌱
varieties.origin: NHRDF. Recommended for Indo-Gangetic plains.
varieties.traits: Light red bulbs. Small-medium with thinner neck.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields. Matures 100-110 days.
varieties.availability: NHRDF, KVK Thrissur, Kerala agricultural suppliers.

varieties.name: Agrifound White 🌱
varieties.origin: NHRDF. White variety for export and processing.
varieties.traits: White bulbs, attractive silvery skin.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields. Evaluated in Thrissur with good performance.
varieties.availability: NHRDF, KVK Thrissur, Kerala agricultural centers.

varieties.name: Agrifound Rose 🌱
varieties.origin: NHRDF. Visually stunning variety.
varieties.traits: Beautiful rose-pink colored bulbs. Visual appeal for garnishes.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields. Specialty variety.
varieties.availability: NHRDF, specialty seed suppliers in Kerala.

varieties.name: Bellary Red 🌱
varieties.origin: Bellary, Karnataka. Famous South Indian variety. Also called Big Onion.
varieties.traits: Large deep red bulbs. Known for robust flavor. Size 25-70mm.
varieties.yield_notes: Good size, excellent keeping quality.

varieties.name: Nasik Red / N-53 🌱
varieties.origin: Nasik, Maharashtra. Famous onion-growing region.
varieties.traits: Flattish round red bulbs, 80-100g. Moderately pungent. Distinct rich flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent size and shape. Matures 150-160 days from sowing.

varieties.name: CO-3 🌱
varieties.origin: Tamil Nadu Agricultural University, Coimbatore.
varieties.traits: Developed for tropical climate. Short day variety.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields in tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: TNAU seed centers, Tamil Nadu and Kerala agricultural stores.

varieties.name: N-2-4-1
varieties.origin: Indian breeding line. Early maturing variety.
varieties.traits: Small-medium bulbs with thin neck. Good for storage.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields for early variety.
varieties.availability: Indian agricultural centers, regional suppliers.

varieties.name: Punjab Red Round
varieties.origin: Punjab, North India.
varieties.traits: Red round bulbs. Small-medium with thin neck.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields.
varieties.availability: Punjab Agricultural University, North Indian seed suppliers, available in Kerala through distributors.

varieties.name: Baswant-780
varieties.origin: Indian variety. Early maturing.
varieties.traits: Early variety for quick turnaround.
varieties.yield_notes: Suitable for moderately hot-dry climates.

varieties.name: Multiplier Onion (Aggregatum) 🌱
varieties.origin: Traditional Indian variety. Also called Cotta pyaas, Aggregatum onion.
varieties.traits: Small bulbs grown in clusters. Propagated through bulblets. Small bulbs grow into large ones which break into smaller ones.
varieties.yield_notes: Multiple bulbs per planting. Self-propagating.
varieties.availability: Kerala local markets, traditional farmers, agricultural centers.

varieties.name: Santra (Multiplier Set) 🌱
varieties.origin: Indian multiplier variety.
varieties.traits: Multiplier set type specifically for monsoon.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields from sets.
varieties.availability: Coastal region suppliers, Kerala agricultural centers.

varieties.name: Kalyanpur White (Multiplier Set) 🌱
varieties.origin: Kalyanpur, India. White multiplier type.
varieties.traits: White multiplier variety for monsoon conditions.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable yields in wet conditions.

varieties.name: Purshu Red (Multiplier Set) 🌱
varieties.origin: Indian red multiplier variety.
varieties.traits: Red multiplier type for tropical monsoon.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable monsoon crop.

varieties.name: VL-1
varieties.origin: Hill variety, India.
varieties.traits: Very early maturing cultivar.
varieties.yield_notes: Quick maturity.
varieties.availability: Hill region agricultural centers, specialized suppliers.

varieties.name: VL-2
varieties.origin: Hill variety, India.
varieties.traits: Very early maturing.
varieties.yield_notes: Quick harvest.
varieties.availability: Hill region suppliers, Kerala highland agricultural stores.

varieties.name: VL-3
varieties.origin: Hill variety for higher elevations 1500-2400m.
varieties.traits: Adapted to higher elevations.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields at elevation.
varieties.availability: Hill region agricultural centers, highland Kerala suppliers.

varieties.name: Punjab Selection
varieties.origin: Punjab Agricultural University.
varieties.traits: Very early maturing.
varieties.yield_notes: Quick turnaround.
varieties.availability: Punjab Agricultural University, North Indian suppliers, available through Kerala distributors.
